instruction: Two issues on the stop icon: the base outline is wrongly tinted blue, AND the red side stripe on top is the wrong color (green). Fix both.
initial_svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 128 128" width="128" height="128">
<rect id="__bg" x="0" y="0" width="128" height="128" fill="white" />
  <g id="icon" data-source="heroicons/outline/stop.svg" transform="translate(8, 8) scale(4.6667)" style="color: #3b82f6" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M5.25 7.5A2.25 2.25 0 0 1 7.5 5.25h9a2.25 2.25 0 0 1 2.25 2.25v9a2.25 2.25 0 0 1-2.25 2.25h-9a2.25 2.25 0 0 1-2.25-2.25v-9Z"/></g>
  <rect id="stripe" fill="#22c55e" x="12" y="16" width="4" height="96" />
</svg>
target_svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 128 128" width="128" height="128">
<rect id="__bg" x="0" y="0" width="128" height="128" fill="white" />
  <g id="icon" data-source="heroicons/outline/stop.svg" transform="translate(8, 8) scale(4.6667)" style="color: #222" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M5.25 7.5A2.25 2.25 0 0 1 7.5 5.25h9a2.25 2.25 0 0 1 2.25 2.25v9a2.25 2.25 0 0 1-2.25 2.25h-9a2.25 2.25 0 0 1-2.25-2.25v-9Z"/></g>
  <rect id="stripe" fill="#e63946" x="12" y="16" width="4" height="96" />
</svg>
